questions.original_question: 路特斯科技在2024年1季度、2季度、3季度的跑车和生活用车交付量分别是多少？
questions.answer:
在2024年，路特斯科技（Lotus Technology）在跑车和生活方式车型的交付量上表现出显著增长。以下是各季度的具体数据：

**第一季度（Q1 2024）**  
- 跑车交付量：1,139辆，相比2023年同期的28辆有显著增长。  
- 生活方式车型交付量：1,041辆，同比增长344%（2023年同期为236辆）。  

**第二季度（Q2 2024）**  
- 跑车交付量：1,345辆，反映了消费者对其高性能跑车的强劲需求。  
- 生活方式车型交付量：1,348辆，展示了公司在全球豪华电动车市场的持续增长。  

**第三季度（Q3 2024）**  
- 跑车交付量：1,150辆，体现了路特斯在全球豪华跑车市场中的强劲表现。  
- 生活方式车型交付量：1,605辆，进一步证明了路特斯科技在全球市场的持续增长势头。  

总体来看，路特斯科技在2024年各季度的交付量均实现了显著增长，反映了其产品策略的成功和全球市场的强劲表现。

rag_info.chunk_content:
Lotus Technology recorded revenue of \$173 million and a gross profit margin of 18%. The company delivered approximately 2,180 vehicles, with global markets outside China contributing over 75% of total deliveries. Lotus Technology revealed luxury customized editions of classic sports cars and battery electric vehicle lifestyle models. New York  
– May 29, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. ("Lotus Technology" or the "Company") (Nasdaq: LOT), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the first quarter ended March 31, 2024. Lotus Technology achieved total revenue of \$173 million in the quarter, a year-on-year growth of 811% and a gross profit margin of 18%, powered by an asset-light model and high-margin businesses including research and development services and accessories. Lotus Technology successfully launched its Chapman Bespoke service in the first quarter of 2024, providing premium customization to Lotus vehicles. Lotus Technology delivered a total of 2,180 vehicles in the first quarter. The Company began deliveries of the Emeya in China, its first electric hyper-grand tourer, and further expanded its product portfolio to four models. In the China market where the Company operates a direct-to-customer sales model, the deliveries in the first quarter had a year-on-year growth of over 100%. Customer deliveries of the Emira, the brand’s internal combustion engine sports car manufactured in the UK, also commenced in the US at the beginning of this year. Global markets outside China contributed over 75% of Lotus Technology's deliveries in the first quarter.

rag_info.chunk_content: New York – January 14, 2025 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the full year of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. delivered 12,065 vehicles in 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of over 70% and boasting the top growth rate among leading luxury brands. China contributed approximately 25% of the total global deliveries in 2024. In 2025, Lotus Technology Inc. aims to increase global deliveries by 20% with a 25-28% contribution from China. Lotus Technology Inc. also successfully reclaimed the proprietary rights of the LOTUS roundel, LOTUS word mark, and the Chinese character of Lotus ("莲花") in China, achieving consistency in terms of its trademark across the world. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market. The vehicle delivery numbers presented above are derived from reports submitted by Lotus Technology Inc.'s business units. These numbers may differ slightly from the vehicle delivery numbers recorded in the financial accounting and reporting systems announced or to be announced by Lotus Technology Inc. Note 2: Including Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren.

(“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the first nine months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 7,543 vehicles in the first nine months of 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of 134%. Global allocation in terms of deliveries remained well-distributed, with Europe, China, North America, and the Rest of the World regions each contributing about 20-35% of the total deliveries. In the third quarter of 2024, the Emeya (GT, Sedan) was delivered to Europe, whilst the Eletre (SUV) reached markets including the GCC, Japan, and Korea. In September, Lotus Technology Inc. revealed the Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant tailored for the North American market with a starting price of $229,900. The deliveries of the Eletre Carbon are expected to commence in the first half of 2025. Deliveries of bespoke and collection versions of both the Eletre (SUV) and the Emeya (GT, Sedan) also commenced in the third quarter.

rag_info.chunk_content: In the first nine months of 2024, Lotus delivered over 7,600 vehicles, marking a 136% increase compared to the same period in 2023, when 3,221 vehicles were delivered. The third quarter alone saw 2,755 vehicles delivered, a 54% increase over the 1,782 vehicles delivered in Q3 2023. Total revenue for the first nine months of 2024 amounted to $653 million, representing an impressive 105% year-on-year growth from the $318 million reported in the same period of 2023. The intelligent driving business showed remarkable growth, with revenue from external customers reaching $11 million, reflecting a year-on-year growth of 450%. Despite this, the gross profit margins declined, with Q3 2024 margins at 3%, down from 15% in Q3 2023. For the first nine months of 2024, the gross profit margin was 9%, compared to 11% in the same period of 2023.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Pursuant to the requirements of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, Lotus Technology has duly caused this report to be signed on its behalf by the undersigned, thereunto duly authorized. NEW YORK, Jan. 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the full year of 2024. Lotus Technology delivered 12,065 vehicles in 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of over 70% and boasting the top growth rate among leading luxury brands. China contributed approximately 25% of the total global deliveries in 2024. In 2025, Lotus Technology aims to increase global deliveries by 20% with a 25-28% contribution from China. Lotus Technology also successfully reclaimed the proprietary rights of the LOTUS roundel, LOTUS word mark, and the Chinese character of Lotus ("莲花") in China, achieving consistency in terms of its trademark across the world. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market. The vehicle delivery numbers presented above are derived from reports submitted by Lotus Technology's business units. These numbers may differ slightly from the vehicle delivery numbers recorded in the financial accounting and reporting systems announced or to be announced by Lotus Technology. Note 2: Including Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren.

This performance established Lotus as the fastest-growing luxury brand, with approximately 25% of deliveries originating from China. Looking ahead, Lotus aims to increase global deliveries by 20% in 2025, with China's contribution expected to rise to 25-28%. The company has also regained proprietary rights to its trademarks, including the iconic LOTUS roundel and the Chinese character for Lotus ("莲花"), reinforcing its global branding consistency. The reported delivery figures encompass invoiced and commissioned deliveries in the US, based on submissions from various business units, and may differ slightly from the company's financial records. metadata.title_summary:
title: Operating Highlights for the First Half of
2024 
summary: In the first half of 2024, the Company achieved a remarkable 239% year-on-year increase in total deliveries, reaching 4,873 units, with significant contributions from various global regions, each accounting for 20-30% of the total. The US market notably represented 26% of these deliveries, reflecting the Company's successful re-entry into the market. 

Deliveries were categorized by model type, with Lifestyle SUVs and Sedans totaling 2,389 units (up 174% from 2023) and Sportscars reaching 2,484 units (a 337% increase). Regionally, Europe led with 1,459 deliveries (30% of total), followed by North America with 1,278 (26%), China with 1,208 (25%), and the Rest of the World with 928 (19%).

The Company is evolving from a traditional sports car manufacturer to an intelligent luxury mobility provider, focusing on technology-related revenue through intelligent driving solutions and R&D services. The introduction of the Chapman Bespoke service caters to growing customer demand for personalized designs, enhancing the brand's luxury appeal and potentially improving long-term gross margins.

In light of changing market conditions and uncertainties related to new tariff policies, the Company has revised its 2024 delivery target to 12,000 units. Additionally, the "Win26" plan has been launched to achieve positive EBITDA by 2026, emphasizing process optimization, cost-saving measures, and product strategy recalibration to better meet the needs of diverse global markets.

Lotus Technology recorded a net loss of US\$724.6 million, US\$750.3 million, and US\$1,107.3 million in 2022, 2023, and 2024, respectively.

title: A. Operating Results

Our product lineup includes a comprehensive range of Lotus-branded cars, reflecting our commitment to quality in the luxury automotive sector. In 2023 and the six months ended June 30, 2024, Lotus Technology delivered a total of 6,970 and 4,873 vehicles, respectively, consisting of lifestyle vehicles and Lotus-brand sports cars that were distributed through Lotus Technology International Limited (LTIL). The growth of the global luxury car segment is expected to outpace that of the overall car market. Within the global battery electric vehicle (BEV) market, the luxury BEV segment is expected to outgrow the mass market BEV segment. Lotus Technology is well positioned to capitalize on the growth momentum of the global luxury BEV segment by leveraging the following strategies.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Business 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Our Business**

Lotus is a premier global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions, renowned for designing and developing luxury lifestyle vehicles under its iconic British brand. Established in the U.K. in 1948, Lotus has a rich heritage of over seven decades in racing, symbolizing excellence in performance, design, and engineering, with a legacy that includes 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles.

In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK, transforming the company into a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer. This strategic partnership enhances Lotus's capabilities in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development (R&D), and logistics, facilitating a transition from a traditional sports car company to a leader in high-performance electric vehicles. The luxury BEV market is currently underserved, with only about 10 existing models compared to over 100 internal combustion engine luxury models, positioning Lotus to capitalize on first-mover advantages.

Lotus launched its first fully electric Hyper-SUV, the Eletre, in 2022, with deliveries commencing in 2023. The Eletre features an advanced 800-volt EPA platform, offering three versions: Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R. The Eletre R achieves an impressive 905 horsepower and accelerates from 0-100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. Its 112-kWh battery provides a WLTP range of 490 km and can be charged from 10% to 80% in under 20 minutes, with an average MSRP exceeding $100,000. Following the Eletre, the second model, the Emeya, was launched in September 2023, with deliveries expected to begin in March 2024.

Lotus's R&D capabilities are a competitive strength, highlighted by the proprietary 800-volt EPA developed over five years, which supports rapid model rollout and production scalability. The company also features advanced ADAS technology, including the first deployable LiDAR system. R&D facilities in Europe and China foster collaboration among experienced teams, enhancing innovation.

All BEV lifestyle models are manufactured through a contract partnership with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art EV manufacturing facilities in Wuhan, China. This partnership enhances scalability and flexibility while minimizing upfront capital commitments. Access to Geely’s global supply chain allows Lotus to secure high-quality components at competitive prices, effectively managing supply chain risks.

Lotus's sales strategy emphasizes a digital-first, omni-channel approach, providing a luxury retail experience through premium stores and a comprehensive digital platform. As of June 30, 2024, the company has established a global distribution network with 207 stores and a Global Commercial Platform for distributing Lotus vehicles, including the Eletre and Emeya. In 2023, Lotus delivered 6,970 vehicles, with 4,873 vehicles delivered in the first half of 2024, encompassing both lifestyle and sports cars.

This comprehensive approach positions Lotus as a leader in the luxury BEV market, leveraging its rich heritage, innovative technology, and strategic partnerships to drive future growth.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Permission, Review and Filing Required from the Authorities in Mainland China Relating to Overseas Offering 
summary: The regulatory landscape for China-based companies seeking to raise capital offshore has become increasingly stringent, reflecting the PRC government's intensified oversight. This trend was notably marked by the issuance of the "Opinions on Severely Cracking Down on Illegal Securities Activities" on July 6, 2021, which underscored the need for enhanced supervision of overseas listings. A key aspect of this oversight involves the establishment of regulatory systems aimed at mitigating risks associated with cybersecurity and data privacy.

Effective February 15, 2022, the revised Measures for Cybersecurity Review require online platform operators that process data impacting national security—specifically those with over one million users—to undergo a cybersecurity review prior to any overseas listing. This regulatory framework is part of a broader effort to ensure that companies comply with national security standards.

On February 17, 2023, the China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC) introduced the Overseas Listing Trial Measures, which mandate that companies, such as Lotus Tech, complete specific filing procedures before pursuing a listing on platforms like Nasdaq. Lotus Tech successfully submitted its filing, which was concluded on February 8, 2024. The Overseas Listing Filing Rules stipulate that issuers must file for various actions, including additional securities issuance and changes in listing status, and are required to report significant events within three business days.

As of the date of the prospectus, Lotus Tech has not encountered any cybersecurity reviews or sanctions from PRC authorities, aside from inquiries related to its filing. Legal counsel for Lotus Tech suggests that while the company believes it must file with the CSRC after issuing American Depositary Shares (ADSs), it does not anticipate needing a cybersecurity review for this specific offering. However, the evolving nature of regulatory interpretation and potential legal changes could result in the need for additional approvals or reviews.

Failure to secure the necessary permissions or misinterpretation of regulatory requirements could lead to significant costs for Lotus Tech and potential sanctions, adversely affecting its business operations, ability to attract foreign investment, and the overall value of its securities. For a comprehensive understanding of the regulatory complexities and their potential impacts on offshore offerings, stakeholders are advised to refer to the "Risk Factors" section of the documentation.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Deliveries (1)  by Model Type 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Vehicle Deliveries by Model Type and Region for first half 2024**

In the first half of 2024, Lotus Tech has reported remarkable growth in vehicle deliveries, achieving a total of 4,873 units, which marks a 239% increase from 1,439 units delivered in the same period of 2023. This growth is particularly notable across various model types and regions, despite facing potential regulatory challenges in China that could impact operations and securities offerings.

**Key Deliveries Data:**
- **Model Types:**
  - **Lifestyle SUVs and Sedans:** 2,389 units delivered, representing a significant 174% increase from 871 units in first half 2023.
  - **Sportscars:** 2,484 units delivered, reflecting a staggering 337% increase from 568 units in first half 2023.
  - **Total Deliveries:** 4,873 units, up from 1,439 units in first half 2023.

- **Regional Performance:**
  - **Europe:** 1,459 units delivered, accounting for 30% of total deliveries, a substantial rise from 89 units in first half 2023.
  - **China:** 1,208 units delivered, making up 25% of total deliveries, an increase from 965 units in first half 2023.
  - **North America:** 1,278 units delivered, representing 26% of total deliveries, with no prior year comparison available.
  - **Rest of the World:** 928 units delivered, contributing 19% to total deliveries, up from 385 units in first half 2023.

**Additional Insights:**
Lotus Tech has expanded its market reach by commencing deliveries of the Eletre to new regions, including the Gulf Cooperation Council, New Zealand, India, and Egypt, while also initiating production orders for Australia, Japan, and Korea. The Emeya has started deliveries in Europe, with ongoing reservations in the Gulf Cooperation Council, Japan, Korea, and Southeast Asia.

The average selling price of vehicles exceeded US$100,000 in the first half of 2024, despite increased competition and rapid growth in the market. In light of evolving market conditions and new tariff policies in the U.S. and EU, Lotus Tech has revised its 2024 delivery target to 12,000 units.

To ensure sustainable growth and profitability, the company has launched the “Win26” plan, aiming for positive EBITDA and operating cash flow by 2026. This plan focuses on process optimization, cost control, and recalibrating the product plan to cater to diversified markets.

Overall, Lotus Tech's performance in first half 2024 showcases its resilience and strategic initiatives to navigate regulatory challenges while capitalizing on market opportunities.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Operational and Financial Summaries 
summary: The operational results for Lotus Tech in the first quarter of 2024 (Q1 2024) reveal significant growth across various metrics compared to the same period in 2023 (Q1 2023). The company saw a remarkable increase in vehicle deliveries, with lifestyle vehicle deliveries soaring to 1,041 units—up 344% from 236 units in Q1 2023—and sportscar deliveries reaching 1,139 units, a substantial rise from just 28 units in the previous year. Overall, combined deliveries for both categories totaled 2,180 units, reflecting an impressive 731% year-over-year growth from 264 units in Q1 2023.

Financially, Lotus Tech reported a revenue of $173 million for Q1 2024, representing an 811% increase from $19 million in Q1 2023. However, the cost of revenues also increased significantly to $143 million, leading to a gross profit of $30 million and a gross profit margin of 18%, up from 1% in the prior year. Despite these gains, the company faced challenges with an operating loss of $233 million and a net loss of $258 million, both of which widened compared to the previous year. Adjusted EBITDA for Q1 2024 was negative $204 million, indicating a decline in profitability.

These results underscore Lotus Tech's strategic expansion into luxury markets and its commitment to innovation under the Vision80 plan, while also highlighting the ongoing challenges in achieving profitability amidst rapid growth and investment in global market engagement.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: New York – Oct 17, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the first nine months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 7,543 vehicles in the first nine months of 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of 134%. Global allocation in terms of deliveries remained well-distributed, with Europe, China, North America, and the Rest of the World regions each contributing about 20-35% of the total deliveries. In the third quarter of 2024, the Emeya (GT, Sedan) was delivered to Europe, whilst the Eletre (SUV) reached markets including the GCC, Japan, and Korea. In September, Lotus Technology Inc. revealed the Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant tailored for the North American market with a starting price of $229,900. The deliveries of the Eletre Carbon are expected to commence in the first half of 2025. Deliveries of bespoke and collection versions of both the Eletre (SUV) and the Emeya (GT, Sedan) also commenced in the third quarter.

Deliveries for this model are anticipated to commence in the first half of 2025. Additionally, bespoke and collection versions of both the Eletre and Emeya began their deliveries in Q3 2024.

Lotus also showcased the Theory 1 concept car in September, highlighting its commitment to advanced technology and eco-friendly features. It is important to note that the delivery figures provided are based on reports from business units and may differ from those recorded in financial accounting systems. This comprehensive overview underscores Lotus Technology's significant growth trajectory and its strategic expansion into various international markets.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 
summary: **Summary of Lotus Technology Inc.'s Financial Performance and Strategic Growth (Q1 2024)**

In the first quarter of 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrated remarkable growth across key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. The company reported revenues of $173 million, reflecting an impressive year-over-year increase of 811%, alongside deliveries of approximately 2,180 vehicles, a 731% rise from 264 units in Q1 2023. The gross profit margin for the quarter was 18%, a significant improvement from just 1% a year prior, although it slightly decreased from the previous quarter's 19%. Despite these gains, the company faced a net loss of $258 million, an increase from a $160 million loss in Q1 2023.

Lotus's strategic initiatives include expanding its global presence, with plans to increase points of sale from 215 in 2023 to over 300 by 2025. The availability of its vehicle models is also set to improve, with the Emira expected to achieve full availability in stores by 2024, while the Eletre and Emeya will follow suit in subsequent years.

Financially, the company has seen substantial growth since 2018, with sales volume skyrocketing from an average of 1,200 units annually to an estimated 26,000 units in 2024. Revenue projections for 2024 are between $2.5 billion and $2.7 billion, up from $679 million in 2023. The gross profit margin is expected to further improve to between 17% and 19%.

As of March 31, 2024, total assets rose to $2.54 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. Current liabilities remained relatively stable, while total liabilities increased to $2.52 billion. Notably, shareholders' equity turned positive, reaching $19.8 million, a significant recovery from a deficit of $1.21 billion at the end of 2023.

Overall, Lotus Technology Inc. is on a robust growth trajectory, marked by significant increases in sales, revenue, and profitability, as well as strategic plans for market expansion and product availability. The company continues to navigate challenges, including net losses and rising operational costs, while focusing on enhancing shareholder value and operational efficiency.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Emeya (Type 133), a four-door luxury and performance vehicle, is Lotus Technology's second lifestyle vehicle and first hyper grand tourer (hyper-GT) vehicle. The Emeya is also one of the world’s most advanced electric hyper-GT vehicles. Lotus Technology expects to start production of the Emeya in 2024. The average MSRP will be higher than US$100,000. The annual sales volume of the Emeya is expected to be between 30,000 and 40,000 units starting from 2027. Future Models Type 134 Type 134 is a D-segment battery electric vehicle (BEV) SUV planned for launch in 2024 with expected delivery in 2026. With an average MSRP higher than US$70,000, Type 134 will be an SUV targeting younger demographics. The annual sales volume of Type 134 is expected to be between 80,000 and 90,000 units starting from 2027. Type 135 Type 135 is a BEV sports car planned for launch in 2025 with expected delivery in 2027. With an average MSRP higher than US$95,000, Type 135 will be Lotus Technology's first all-electric sports car, built upon Lotus’s unique sports car platform. The expected annual sales volume of Type 135 is projected to be between 10,000 and 15,000 units starting from 2029.

In addition, the Type 134 is a conceptual D-segment battery electric SUV set to launch in 2024, targeting younger consumers with an anticipated MSRP exceeding $70,000 and projected annual sales of 80,000 to 90,000 units starting in 2027. Lastly, the Type 135 is an all-electric sports car, Lotus's first BEV sports model, expected to launch in 2025 with deliveries in 2027, priced above $95,000 and projected to sell between 10,000 and 15,000 units annually starting in 2029.

metadata.title_summary:
title: •  Projected vehicle related revenue: 
summary: Lotus Tech is projecting significant growth in vehicle sales, anticipating to exceed 73,000 units by 2025, which represents a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 185% from 2023 to 2025. This growth is primarily driven by the sales of its models, including the Eletre, Emeya, Emira, and Evija. The company expects to sell approximately 9,000 vehicles in 2023, with a notable sales distribution from China (55%), Europe (30%), and the US (3%). By 2024, sales are projected to rise to around 47,000 vehicles, and by 2025, to over 73,000, with a more balanced contribution from key markets.

Financially, Lotus Tech anticipates a rise in gross profit margins from an estimated 8%–10% in 2023 to 21%–23% by 2025, despite a downward revision in initial gross margin estimates due to increased raw material costs. The company also expects to achieve a positive EBITDA margin exceeding 4% by 2025, driven by improved gross profit margins and reduced operating expenses. However, the company faces challenges, including potential delays in vehicle deliveries due to customs issues and the European Commission's anti-subsidy investigation affecting pricing strategies in Europe.

Lotus Tech's financial projections include securing $223 million from the Trust Account and an additional $500 million through fundraising, but there are risks associated with meeting these financial targets. The company has indicated a cash outflow exceeding $1 billion from operating and investing activities in 2023, highlighting the need for careful financial management moving forward.

Overall, Lotus Tech's projections reflect a strong growth trajectory in the luxury battery electric vehicle market, with a focus on expanding sales and improving profitability, while also navigating potential market and operational challenges.

title: •  Projected vehicle related revenue: 
summary: Lotus Tech is projecting significant growth in vehicle sales, expecting to exceed 73,000 units by 2025, which translates to a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 185% from 2023 to 2025. This growth is primarily driven by the sales of its models, including the Eletre, Emeya, Emira, and Evija. The company's sales forecasts for 2023 estimate around 9,000 vehicles, with the Eletre model contributing approximately 6,000 units. By 2024, sales are expected to rise to about 47,000 vehicles, and by 2025, they are projected to surpass 73,000 units, with a notable increase in the contribution from the Eletre and Emeya models.

The anticipated sales distribution highlights a strong focus on markets in China, Europe, and the United States, with China expected to account for a significant portion of sales. The company also plans to adjust pricing for its models in Europe due to potential tariff increases linked to an anti-subsidy investigation into Chinese battery electric vehicle (BEV) imports.

Financially, Lotus Tech expects revenues to grow from an estimated $0.9 billion to $1.2 billion in 2023, reaching between $8.2 billion and $8.6 billion by 2025. Gross profit is projected to increase from $0.1 billion to $0.2 billion in 2023 to between $1.7 billion and $1.9 billion in 2025, with gross margins improving from 8%-10% in 2023 to 21%-23% by 2025. Despite some challenges, including potential delivery delays and increased raw material costs, the company anticipates achieving a positive EBITDA margin exceeding 4% by 2025.

Lotus Tech's projections are based on several assumptions regarding market potential, model launch timelines, and the effectiveness of its sales network. However, the company acknowledges the inherent uncertainties in these forecasts and advises caution in relying solely on the projected figures. Overall, Lotus Tech's outlook reflects a robust growth trajectory in the luxury BEV segment, driven by strong demand for its innovative vehicle offerings.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Certain Prospective Operational and Financial Information 
summary: On November 28, 2022, Lotus Tech provided LCAA with initial financial forecasts covering revenue, gross profit, and EBITDA margin estimates for 2023 to 2025, which were prepared without adherence to SEC guidelines or U.S. GAAP. These projections were intended for internal use to assist LCAA's due diligence prior to the Business Combination approval. Subsequent updates were shared in June and October 2023, collectively referred to as the "Financial Projections." 

Lotus Tech emphasizes that these projections are subjective and based on various assumptions, many of which are uncertain and may change over time, potentially leading to significant discrepancies between projected and actual results. The company does not publicly disclose such forecasts and does not intend to do so in the future, highlighting that the projections should not be relied upon as guarantees of future performance.

The Financial Projections indicate a growth trajectory, with revenues expected to rise from approximately $2.2 billion to $2.5 billion in 2023, reaching between $6.2 billion and $6.6 billion in 2024, and further increasing to between $8.5 billion and $8.9 billion in 2025. Gross profit is projected to grow from $0.3 billion to $0.4 billion in 2023 to between $1.8 billion and $2.0 billion by 2025, with gross margins improving from 14%–16% to 21%–23% over the same period.

Lotus Tech anticipates vehicle sales will exceed 76,000 units by 2025, driven by models such as the Eletre and Type 133, with significant contributions from both vehicle-related and technology-related revenues. The company also projects growth in ecosystem-related revenue through charging services and peripheral product sales. However, stakeholders are cautioned about the inherent risks and uncertainties that could impact these forecasts, including market conditions, competitive pressures, and operational challenges.

Overall, while Lotus Tech's management has outlined optimistic projections based on reasonable assumptions, the company acknowledges the potential for actual results to vary significantly from these estimates, underscoring the need for caution among investors and stakeholders.

Overall, Lotus Technology Inc. is navigating a complex landscape of rapid growth and financial challenges, while maintaining its legacy in automotive excellence and motorsports. metadata.title_summary:
title: Financial Highlights for the First Quarter of 2025 
summary: In the first quarter of 2025, the company reported total revenues of $93 million, a significant decline of 46% compared to $173 million in the same period of 2024. Despite this drop, the gross margin showed some improvement, rising to 12% from 18% in Q1 2024, although gross profit fell sharply from $30 million to $11 million. The cost of revenues also decreased, down 43% from $143 million to $82 million.

The operating loss improved significantly, decreasing by 56% from $233 million in Q1 2024 to $103 million in Q1 2025. Similarly, the net loss reduced by 29%, from $258 million to $183 million. Adjusted EBITDA also reflected a positive trend, with losses improving from $204 million to $136 million, marking a 33% year-over-year enhancement. 

Overall, the financial results indicate a challenging environment with reduced revenues and profitability, yet improvements in operating loss and adjusted metrics suggest a potential recovery trajectory. The company is also pursuing new initiatives, including launching a GT racing series in China and Malaysia, releasing an ESG report, and introducing a new PHEV model. For a comprehensive understanding of the financial performance, readers are encouraged to refer to the sections on non-GAAP financial measures and reconciliations.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Pursuant to the requirements of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, Lotus Technology has duly caused this report to be signed on its behalf by the undersigned, thereunto duly authorized. NEW YORK, Jan. 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the full year of 2024. Lotus Technology delivered 12,065 vehicles in 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of over 70% and boasting the top growth rate among leading luxury brands. China contributed approximately 25% of the total global deliveries in 2024. In 2025, Lotus Technology aims to increase global deliveries by 20% with a 25-28% contribution from China. Lotus Technology also successfully reclaimed the proprietary rights of the LOTUS roundel, LOTUS word mark, and the Chinese character of Lotus ("莲花") in China, achieving consistency in terms of its trademark across the world. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market. The vehicle delivery numbers presented above are derived from reports submitted by Lotus Technology's business units. These numbers may differ slightly from the vehicle delivery numbers recorded in the financial accounting and reporting systems announced or to be announced by Lotus Technology. Note 2: Including Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren.

Looking ahead, Lotus aims to increase global deliveries by 20% in 2025, with China's contribution expected to rise to 25-28%. Lotus Technology has established a successful track record since its inception. In 2024, Lotus Technology delivered a total of 12,134 vehicles, which consisted of its lifestyle vehicles and sportscars distributed through Lotus Technology International Limited (LTIL). As of December 31, 2024, Lotus Technology had 1,996 full-time employees globally, including in China and Europe, among which approximately 52% belong to its R&D division. Lotus Technology's total revenues increased from US\$9.6 million for the year ended December 31, 2022, to US\$679.0 million for the year ended December 31, 2023, and again to US\$924.3 million for the year ended December 31, 2024. Lotus Technology recorded a net loss of US\$724.6 million, US\$750.3 million, and US\$1,107.3 million in 2022, 2023, and 2024, respectively.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: New York – November 12, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 8,631 vehicles in the first ten months of 2024. Europe and China continued to be the major markets for Lotus Technology, with each region contributing about 35% and 25% of the total deliveries, respectively. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. started an open beta test for its urban NOA (Navigate on Autopilot) in key cities. Certain over-the-air updates of intelligent driving, including highway assistant driving functions, were launched in Europe. As previously announced, Lotus Robotics, Lotus Technology's intelligent driving arm, provides intelligent driving solutions with global coverage to both Lotus-brand intelligent vehicles and other global leading auto conglomerates such as Lynk&Co, Farizon Auto, and a top Japanese Tier-one supplier. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. was awarded the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category as the only awardee from the automotive industry. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: As of June 30, 2024, Lotus Technology had 207 stores in its global distribution network, and it plans to continue expanding its retail network. In 2023 and the six months ended June 30, 2024, Lotus Technology delivered a total of 6,970 and 4,873 vehicles, respectively, consisting of lifestyle vehicles and Lotus-brand sports cars that were distributed through Lotus Technology International Limited (LTIL). The growth of the global luxury car segment is expected to outpace that of the overall car market. Within the global battery electric vehicle (BEV) market, the luxury BEV segment is expected to outgrow the mass market BEV segment. Lotus Technology is well positioned to capitalize on the growth momentum of the global luxury BEV segment by leveraging the following strategies.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Business 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Our Business**

Lotus is a premier global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions, renowned for designing and developing luxury lifestyle vehicles under its iconic British brand. Established in the U.K. in 1948, Lotus has a rich heritage of over seven decades in racing, symbolizing excellence in performance, design, and engineering, with a legacy that includes 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles.

In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK, transforming the company into a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer. This strategic partnership enhances Lotus's capabilities in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development (R&D), and logistics, facilitating a transition from a traditional sports car company to a leader in high-performance electric vehicles. The luxury BEV market is currently underserved, with only about 10 existing models compared to over 100 internal combustion engine luxury models, positioning Lotus to capitalize on first-mover advantages.

Lotus launched its first fully electric Hyper-SUV, the Eletre, in 2022, with deliveries commencing in 2023. The Eletre features an advanced 800-volt EPA platform, offering three versions: Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R. The Eletre R achieves an impressive 905 horsepower and accelerates from 0-100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. Its 112-kWh battery provides a WLTP range of 490 km and can be charged from 10% to 80% in under 20 minutes, with an average MSRP exceeding $100,000. Following the Eletre, the second model, the Emeya, was launched in September 2023, with deliveries expected to begin in March 2024.

Lotus's R&D capabilities are a competitive strength, highlighted by the proprietary 800-volt EPA developed over five years, which supports rapid model rollout and production scalability. The company also features advanced ADAS technology, including the first deployable LiDAR system. R&D facilities in Europe and China foster collaboration among experienced teams, enhancing innovation.

All BEV lifestyle models are manufactured through a contract partnership with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art EV manufacturing facilities in Wuhan, China. This partnership enhances scalability and flexibility while minimizing upfront capital commitments. Access to Geely’s global supply chain allows Lotus to secure high-quality components at competitive prices, effectively managing supply chain risks.

Lotus's sales strategy emphasizes a digital-first, omni-channel approach, providing a luxury retail experience through premium stores and a comprehensive digital platform. As of June 30, 2024, the company has established a global distribution network with 207 stores and a Global Commercial Platform for distributing Lotus vehicles, including the Eletre and Emeya. In 2023, Lotus delivered 6,970 vehicles, with 4,873 vehicles delivered in the first half of 2024, encompassing both lifestyle and sports cars.

This comprehensive approach positions Lotus as a leader in the luxury BEV market, leveraging its rich heritage, innovative technology, and strategic partnerships to drive future growth.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Operational and Financial Summaries 
summary: The operational results for Lotus Tech for the first quarter of 2024 (1Q 2024) reveal substantial growth across various performance metrics compared to the same period in 2023 (1Q 2023). Key highlights include a dramatic increase in vehicle deliveries, with total units reaching 2,180, marking a 731% rise from 264 units in 1Q 2023. Notably, lifestyle vehicle deliveries surged to 1,041 units, up 344% from 236 units, while sportscar deliveries rose to 1,139 units from just 28 units, reflecting strong demand and customer preferences.

Financially, Lotus Tech reported revenues of 173 million in 1Q 2024, an impressive 811% increase from 19 million in the previous year, with total revenue for FY2023 at 679 million. However, the cost of revenues also escalated to 143 million, a 653% increase, leading to a gross profit of 30 million and a gross margin of 18%. Despite these positive trends, the company faced challenges with an operating loss of 233 million, a 44% increase from the prior year, and a net loss of 258 million, up 61%. Adjusted EBITDA also declined to -204 million.

Overall, while Lotus Tech has demonstrated significant growth in deliveries and revenue, it continues to experience substantial operating and net losses as it advances its Vision80 plan for international expansion and innovation. The performance indicators underscore both the potential and the challenges the organization faces in a competitive market.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Operating Highlights for the First Half of
2024 
summary: In the first half of 2024, the Company achieved a remarkable 239% year-on-year increase in total deliveries, reaching 4,873 units, with significant contributions from various global regions, each accounting for 20-30% of the total. The US market notably represented 26% of these deliveries, reflecting the Company's successful re-entry into the market. 

Deliveries were categorized by model type, with Lifestyle SUVs and Sedans totaling 2,389 units (up 174% from 2023) and Sportscars reaching 2,484 units (a 337% increase). Regionally, Europe led with 1,459 deliveries (30% of total), followed by North America with 1,278 (26%), China with 1,208 (25%), and the Rest of the World with 928 (19%).

The Company is evolving from a traditional sports car manufacturer to an intelligent luxury mobility provider, focusing on technology-related revenue through intelligent driving solutions and R&D services. The introduction of the Chapman Bespoke service caters to growing customer demand for personalized designs, enhancing the brand's luxury appeal and potentially improving long-term gross margins.

In light of changing market conditions and uncertainties related to new tariff policies, the Company has revised its 2024 delivery target to 12,000 units. Additionally, the "Win26" plan has been launched to achieve positive EBITDA by 2026, emphasizing process optimization, cost-saving measures, and product strategy recalibration to better meet the needs of diverse global markets.

summary: In 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. all_retrieved_content.page_content: Deliveries of lifestyle vehicles achieved a 57% year-over-year increase in the year of 2024, contributing 57% of total deliveries. metadata.title_summary:
title: 4TH QUARTER AND FULL YEAR 2024 KEY NUMBERS 
summary: In 2024, Lotus achieved a remarkable 57% year-over-year increase in lifestyle vehicle deliveries, which constituted 57% of its total deliveries. The brand's home market, Europe, experienced exceptional growth of 179%, contributing nearly 40% to total deliveries. Despite increased competition and a challenging economic landscape, China remained a significant market, accounting for 25% of total deliveries. North America saw an extraordinary surge of 415% in deliveries, primarily driven by the launch of the Emira model, representing 21% of global deliveries. Additionally, the Rest of the World region contributed 15% to total deliveries, with nearly 90% year-on-year growth.

In terms of financial performance for FY2024, Lotus delivered 12,134 units, a 74% increase from 6,970 units in FY2023, with fourth-quarter deliveries rising by 20% to 4,506 units. Revenue for FY2024 reached $924 million, up 36% from $679 million in FY2023, although fourth-quarter revenue declined by 25% to $272 million. The cost of revenues increased significantly to $895 million, a 55% rise from the previous year, with fourth-quarter costs up 3% to $301 million.

Gross profit for FY2024 fell to $29 million, a 71% decrease, and the fourth quarter saw a gross loss of $29 million, contrasting with a profit in the same quarter the previous year. The gross profit margin dropped to 3% for FY2024, down from 15% in FY2023, with a negative margin of 11% in the fourth quarter. Operating losses for FY2024 reached $786 million, while the net loss nearly doubled in the fourth quarter to $441 million.

Overall, while Lotus demonstrated impressive growth in unit deliveries and revenue, the financial metrics revealed significant challenges, particularly in profitability, indicating a need for strategic adjustments moving forward.

Lotus Technology recorded revenue of \$173 million and a gross profit margin of 18%. The company delivered approximately 2,180 vehicles, with global markets outside China contributing over 75% of total deliveries. Lotus Technology revealed luxury customized editions of classic sports cars and battery electric vehicle lifestyle models. New York  
– May 29, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. ("Lotus Technology" or the "Company") (Nasdaq: LOT), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the first quarter ended March 31, 2024. Lotus Technology achieved total revenue of \$173 million in the quarter, a year-on-year growth of 811% and a gross profit margin of 18%, powered by an asset-light model and high-margin businesses including research and development services and accessories. Lotus Technology successfully launched its Chapman Bespoke service in the first quarter of 2024, providing premium customization to Lotus vehicles. Lotus Technology delivered a total of 2,180 vehicles in the first quarter. The Company began deliveries of the Emeya in China, its first electric hyper-grand tourer, and further expanded its product portfolio to four models. In the China market where the Company operates a direct-to-customer sales model, the deliveries in the first quarter had a year-on-year growth of over 100%. Customer deliveries of the Emira, the brand’s internal combustion engine sports car manufactured in the UK, also commenced in the US at the beginning of this year. Global markets outside China contributed over 75% of Lotus Technology's deliveries in the first quarter.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology
Reports Unaudited First Quarter 2024 Financial Result 
summary: Lotus Tech, a leading luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, reported impressive financial results for the first quarter of 2024, with revenues reaching $173 million, reflecting an 811% year-on-year growth and a gross profit margin of 18%. The company delivered 2,180 vehicles, with over 75% of these sales occurring in global markets outside of China. This quarter marked the launch of the Emeya, Lotus's first electric hyper-grand tourer, and the introduction of the Chapman Bespoke service, which offers premium customization options for its vehicles. The company's direct-to-customer sales model in China led to over 100% growth in deliveries, while the Emira, a traditional sports car, began deliveries in the US. With a robust global sales network of over 200 stores, Lotus Tech is expanding its reach into new markets across the Middle East, Asia, and the Americas, and plans to introduce additional models throughout the year. CEO Qingfeng Feng emphasized the company's commitment to innovation and its Vision80 plan, highlighting the effectiveness of its global strategy in driving growth in key luxury markets.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: With a global sales network of over 200 stores in prime locations around the world, Lotus Technology continues to leverage its global distribution footprint for international expansion. After China and Europe, Lotus Technology began taking orders for the Eletre in more than 10 new markets across the Middle East, Asia, and the Americas in the first quarter. Lotus Technology also expects to begin expanding Emeya sales to new markets in the third quarter of the year. "Through Lotus Technology's unwavering commitment to innovation and excellence, Lotus Technology is making steady progress on delivering Lotus Tech’s Vision80 plan," said Lotus Technology CEO Qingfeng Feng. "Lotus Technology's global strategy continues to drive growth and success as the company expands its presence and sales across key luxury markets worldwide. Lotus Technology is encouraged by the positive feedback received in these new markets and looks forward to rolling out new models to new geographies throughout the year."

metadata.title_summary:
title: Deliveries by model type 
summary: In Q4 2023, vehicle sales surged to 3,749 units, a remarkable 110% increase from Q3's 1,782 units, primarily driven by a 191% rise in lifestyle vehicle sales, which jumped from 891 to 2,599 units. Sportscar sales also increased by 29%, from 891 units in Q3 to 1,150 in Q4. For the entire year, total vehicle sales reached 6,970 units, with lifestyle vehicles accounting for 4,361 units and sportscars for 2,609 units.

The "Regional Deliveries Comparison" highlights significant growth in deliveries across various regions from December 31, 2022, to December 31, 2023. North America saw deliveries rise from 41 to 47, Europe from 54 to 70, and China from 41 to 65, while deliveries in the Rest of the World remained steady at 33. Overall, total deliveries increased from 169 to 215, showcasing the company's successful expansion efforts.

Financially, Lotus Tech reported substantial growth in revenue and gross profit in 2023, particularly in Q4, where revenue reached $361 million, a 92% increase from Q3's $188 million. The total revenue for the year was $679 million. However, the cost of sales also rose to $293 million in Q4, leading to a gross profit of $68 million, up 142% from Q3. Despite these gains, the company faced significant operating and net losses, with an operating loss of $228 million in Q4 and a total net loss of $750 million for the year. Adjusted EBITDA also worsened, reflecting ongoing challenges in executing its Vision80 strategy amidst financial pressures.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 
summary: **Summary of Lotus Technology Inc.'s Financial Performance and Strategic Growth (Q1 2024)**

In the first quarter of 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrated remarkable growth across key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. The company reported revenues of $173 million, reflecting an impressive year-over-year increase of 811%, alongside deliveries of approximately 2,180 vehicles, a 731% rise from 264 units in Q1 2023. The gross profit margin for the quarter was 18%, a significant improvement from just 1% a year prior, although it slightly decreased from the previous quarter's 19%. Despite these gains, the company faced a net loss of $258 million, an increase from a $160 million loss in Q1 2023.

Lotus's strategic initiatives include expanding its global presence, with plans to increase points of sale from 215 in 2023 to over 300 by 2025. The availability of its vehicle models is also set to improve, with the Emira expected to achieve full availability in stores by 2024, while the Eletre and Emeya will follow suit in subsequent years.

Financially, the company has seen substantial growth since 2018, with sales volume skyrocketing from an average of 1,200 units annually to an estimated 26,000 units in 2024. Revenue projections for 2024 are between $2.5 billion and $2.7 billion, up from $679 million in 2023. The gross profit margin is expected to further improve to between 17% and 19%.

As of March 31, 2024, total assets rose to $2.54 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. Current liabilities remained relatively stable, while total liabilities increased to $2.52 billion. Notably, shareholders' equity turned positive, reaching $19.8 million, a significant recovery from a deficit of $1.21 billion at the end of 2023.

Overall, Lotus Technology Inc. is on a robust growth trajectory, marked by significant increases in sales, revenue, and profitability, as well as strategic plans for market expansion and product availability. The company continues to navigate challenges, including net losses and rising operational costs, while focusing on enhancing shareholder value and operational efficiency.

summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has demonstrated significant growth and a strong commitment to expanding its global presence, as evidenced by its financial performance and strategic initiatives. The company reported an impressive 811% increase in revenue year-over-year for the first quarter of 2024, reaching $173 million, driven by a substantial rise in vehicle deliveries. This growth trajectory is supported by a shift towards higher-margin operations and an asset-light business model.

The brand's legacy in motorsports is highlighted by its historical achievements, including multiple championships and prestigious race wins, reinforcing its reputation in the automotive industry. Lotus is also focused on expanding its sales network, with plans to increase points of sale from 215 in 2023 to over 300 by 2025, alongside enhancing vehicle availability for models like the Emira and Eletre.

Despite these advancements, Lotus Technology faces challenges reflected in its financial statements, including a net loss of $258 million in Q1 2024, which marks a significant increase from the previous year. The company’s balance sheet shows a notable rise in total assets, alongside increased liabilities, indicating ongoing investments and operational costs.

Overall, Lotus Technology Inc. is navigating a complex landscape of rapid growth and financial challenges, while maintaining its legacy in automotive excellence and motorsports. The company's strategic focus on expanding its market presence and improving operational efficiency positions it for potential future success, despite current financial hurdles.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Certain Prospective Operational and Financial Information 
summary: On November 28, 2022, Lotus Tech provided LCAA with initial financial projections for 2023 to 2025, including revenue, gross profit, and EBITDA margin estimates, to assist in their due diligence process prior to the approval of a business combination. These projections were updated in June and October 2023, collectively referred to as the "Financial Projections." It is important to note that these projections were not prepared in accordance with SEC guidelines or GAAP and were intended for internal use, reflecting management's expectations and assumptions about future performance, which may be subject to significant uncertainties.

The Financial Projections indicate a strong growth trajectory, with projected revenues for 2023 ranging from approximately $2.2 billion to $2.5 billion, increasing to $6.2 billion to $6.6 billion in 2024, and reaching $8.5 billion to $8.9 billion by 2025. Gross profit is expected to rise from $0.3 billion to $0.4 billion in 2023 to $1.8 billion to $2.0 billion by 2025, while gross margins are projected to improve from 14.0%–16.0% in 2023 to 21.0%–23.0% in 2025.

Lotus Tech anticipates selling over 76,000 vehicles by 2025, driven by several key models, including the Eletre and Type 133. The company expects significant contributions from vehicle sales, technology-related revenue, and ecosystem services. However, the projections carry inherent risks, including potential delays in vehicle launches and market dynamics, which could lead to discrepancies between projected and actual results.

Lotus Tech emphasizes that the Financial Projections should not be relied upon as definitive guidance, as they are speculative and subject to numerous uncertainties. The company does not guarantee the accuracy of these forecasts and will not reference them in future reports post-business combination. The projections were prepared by management and have not been audited by KPMG Huazhen LLP, which only provided assurance on historical financial information. Overall, while the projections suggest a promising outlook for Lotus Tech, stakeholders are advised to approach them with caution due to the various risks and assumptions involved.

These projections were intended for internal use to assist LCAA's due diligence prior to the Business Combination approval. Subsequent updates were shared in June and October 2023, collectively referred to as the "Financial Projections." 

Lotus Tech emphasizes that these projections are subjective and based on various assumptions, many of which are uncertain and may change over time, potentially leading to significant discrepancies between projected and actual results. The company does not publicly disclose such forecasts and does not intend to do so in the future, highlighting that the projections should not be relied upon as guarantees of future performance.

The Financial Projections indicate a growth trajectory, with revenues expected to rise from approximately $2.2 billion to $2.5 billion in 2023, reaching between $6.2 billion and $6.6 billion in 2024, and further increasing to between $8.5 billion and $8.9 billion in 2025. Gross profit is projected to grow from $0.3 billion to $0.4 billion in 2023 to between $1.8 billion and $2.0 billion by 2025, with gross margins improving from 14%–16% to 21%–23% over the same period.

Lotus Tech anticipates vehicle sales will exceed 76,000 units by 2025, driven by models such as the Eletre and Type 133, with significant contributions from both vehicle-related and technology-related revenues. The company also projects growth in ecosystem-related revenue through charging services and peripheral product sales. However, stakeholders are cautioned about the inherent risks and uncertainties that could impact these forecasts, including market conditions, competitive pressures, and operational challenges.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Financial Highlights for the First Quarter of 2025 
summary: In the first quarter of 2025, the company reported total revenues of $93 million, a significant decline of 46% compared to $173 million in the same period of 2024. Despite this drop, the gross margin showed some improvement, rising to 12% from 18% in Q1 2024, although gross profit fell sharply from $30 million to $11 million. The cost of revenues also decreased, down 43% from $143 million to $82 million.

The operating loss improved significantly, decreasing by 56% from $233 million in Q1 2024 to $103 million in Q1 2025. Similarly, the net loss reduced by 29%, from $258 million to $183 million. Adjusted EBITDA also reflected a positive trend, with losses improving from $204 million to $136 million, marking a 33% year-over-year enhancement. 

Overall, the financial results indicate a challenging environment with reduced revenues and profitability, yet improvements in operating loss and adjusted metrics suggest a potential recovery trajectory. The company is also pursuing new initiatives, including launching a GT racing series in China and Malaysia, releasing an ESG report, and introducing a new PHEV model. For a comprehensive understanding of the financial performance, readers are encouraged to refer to the sections on non-GAAP financial measures and reconciliations.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Pursuant to the requirements of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, Lotus Technology has duly caused this report to be signed on its behalf by the undersigned, thereunto duly authorized. NEW YORK, Jan. 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the full year of 2024. Lotus Technology delivered 12,065 vehicles in 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of over 70% and boasting the top growth rate among leading luxury brands. China contributed approximately 25% of the total global deliveries in 2024. In 2025, Lotus Technology aims to increase global deliveries by 20% with a 25-28% contribution from China. Lotus Technology also successfully reclaimed the proprietary rights of the LOTUS roundel, LOTUS word mark, and the Chinese character of Lotus ("莲花") in China, achieving consistency in terms of its trademark across the world. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market. The vehicle delivery numbers presented above are derived from reports submitted by Lotus Technology's business units. These numbers may differ slightly from the vehicle delivery numbers recorded in the financial accounting and reporting systems announced or to be announced by Lotus Technology. Note 2: Including Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren.

This performance established Lotus as the fastest-growing luxury brand, with approximately 25% of deliveries originating from China. Looking ahead, Lotus aims to increase global deliveries by 20% in 2025, with China's contribution expected to rise to 25-28%. The company has also regained proprietary rights to its trademarks, including the iconic LOTUS roundel and the Chinese character for Lotus ("莲花"), reinforcing its global branding consistency. The reported delivery figures encompass invoiced and commissioned deliveries in the US, based on submissions from various business units, and may differ slightly from the company's financial records. Additionally, the summary highlights the competitive landscape, referencing prestigious luxury automotive brands such as Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren, known for their exceptional craftsmanship and appeal to elite car enthusiasts.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: New York – November 12, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 8,631 vehicles in the first ten months of 2024. Europe and China continued to be the major markets for Lotus Technology, with each region contributing about 35% and 25% of the total deliveries, respectively. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. started an open beta test for its urban NOA (Navigate on Autopilot) in key cities. Certain over-the-air updates of intelligent driving, including highway assistant driving functions, were launched in Europe. As previously announced, Lotus Robotics, Lotus Technology's intelligent driving arm, provides intelligent driving solutions with global coverage to both Lotus-brand intelligent vehicles and other global leading auto conglomerates such as Lynk&Co, Farizon Auto, and a top Japanese Tier-one supplier. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. was awarded the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category as the only awardee from the automotive industry. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market.

Additionally, Lotus Robotics, the company's division focused on intelligent driving solutions, is providing services not only for Lotus vehicles but also for other major automotive manufacturers, including Lynk&Co and Farizon Auto. Overall, Lotus Technology continues to make significant strides in vehicle delivery, technological advancements, and sustainability initiatives.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: As of June 30, 2024, Lotus Technology had 207 stores in its global distribution network, and it plans to continue expanding its retail network. In 2023 and the six months ended June 30, 2024, Lotus Technology delivered a total of 6,970 and 4,873 vehicles, respectively, consisting of lifestyle vehicles and Lotus-brand sports cars that were distributed through Lotus Technology International Limited (LTIL). The growth of the global luxury car segment is expected to outpace that of the overall car market. Within the global battery electric vehicle (BEV) market, the luxury BEV segment is expected to outgrow the mass market BEV segment. Lotus Technology is well positioned to capitalize on the growth momentum of the global luxury BEV segment by leveraging the following strategies.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Business 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Our Business**

Lotus is a premier global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions, renowned for designing and developing luxury lifestyle vehicles under its iconic British brand. Established in the U.K. in 1948, Lotus has a rich heritage of over seven decades in racing, symbolizing excellence in performance, design, and engineering, with a legacy that includes 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles.

In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK, transforming the company into a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer. This strategic partnership enhances Lotus's capabilities in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development (R&D), and logistics, facilitating a transition from a traditional sports car company to a leader in high-performance electric vehicles. The luxury BEV market is currently underserved, with only about 10 existing models compared to over 100 internal combustion engine luxury models, positioning Lotus to capitalize on first-mover advantages.

Lotus launched its first fully electric Hyper-SUV, the Eletre, in 2022, with deliveries commencing in 2023. The Eletre features an advanced 800-volt EPA platform, offering three versions: Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R. The Eletre R achieves an impressive 905 horsepower and accelerates from 0-100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. Its 112-kWh battery provides a WLTP range of 490 km and can be charged from 10% to 80% in under 20 minutes, with an average MSRP exceeding $100,000. Following the Eletre, the second model, the Emeya, was launched in September 2023, with deliveries expected to begin in March 2024.

Lotus's R&D capabilities are a competitive strength, highlighted by the proprietary 800-volt EPA developed over five years, which supports rapid model rollout and production scalability. The company also features advanced ADAS technology, including the first deployable LiDAR system. R&D facilities in Europe and China foster collaboration among experienced teams, enhancing innovation.

All BEV lifestyle models are manufactured through a contract partnership with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art EV manufacturing facilities in Wuhan, China. This partnership enhances scalability and flexibility while minimizing upfront capital commitments. Access to Geely’s global supply chain allows Lotus to secure high-quality components at competitive prices, effectively managing supply chain risks.

Lotus's sales strategy emphasizes a digital-first, omni-channel approach, providing a luxury retail experience through premium stores and a comprehensive digital platform. As of June 30, 2024, the company has established a global distribution network with 207 stores and a Global Commercial Platform for distributing Lotus vehicles, including the Eletre and Emeya. In 2023, Lotus delivered 6,970 vehicles, with 4,873 vehicles delivered in the first half of 2024, encompassing both lifestyle and sports cars.

This comprehensive approach positions Lotus as a leader in the luxury BEV market, leveraging its rich heritage, innovative technology, and strategic partnerships to drive future growth.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology is a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider that designs and develops luxury lifestyle vehicles (non-sports cars for daily usage) under the iconic British brand “Lotus” and sells all Lotus branded cars. Lotus Technology has established a successful track record since its inception. In 2024, Lotus Technology delivered a total of 12,134 vehicles, which consisted of its lifestyle vehicles and sportscars distributed through Lotus Technology International Limited (LTIL). As of December 31, 2024, Lotus Technology had 1,996 full-time employees globally, including in China and Europe, among which approximately 52% belong to its R&D division. Lotus Technology's total revenues increased from US\$9.6 million for the year ended December 31, 2022, to US\$679.0 million for the year ended December 31, 2023, and again to US\$924.3 million for the year ended December 31, 2024. Lotus Technology recorded a net loss of US\$724.6 million, US\$750.3 million, and US\$1,107.3 million in 2022, 2023, and 2024, respectively.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Operating Highlights for the First Half of
2024 
summary: In the first half of 2024, the Company achieved a remarkable 239% year-on-year increase in total deliveries, reaching 4,873 units, with significant contributions from various global regions, each accounting for 20-30% of the total. The US market notably represented 26% of these deliveries, reflecting the Company's successful re-entry into the market. 

Deliveries were categorized by model type, with Lifestyle SUVs and Sedans totaling 2,389 units (up 174% from 2023) and Sportscars reaching 2,484 units (a 337% increase). Regionally, Europe led with 1,459 deliveries (30% of total), followed by North America with 1,278 (26%), China with 1,208 (25%), and the Rest of the World with 928 (19%).

The Company is evolving from a traditional sports car manufacturer to an intelligent luxury mobility provider, focusing on technology-related revenue through intelligent driving solutions and R&D services. The introduction of the Chapman Bespoke service caters to growing customer demand for personalized designs, enhancing the brand's luxury appeal and potentially improving long-term gross margins.

In light of changing market conditions and uncertainties related to new tariff policies, the Company has revised its 2024 delivery target to 12,000 units. Additionally, the "Win26" plan has been launched to achieve positive EBITDA by 2026, emphasizing process optimization, cost-saving measures, and product strategy recalibration to better meet the needs of diverse global markets.

As of June 30, 2024, Lotus Technology had 2,989 full-time employees globally, including in China and Europe, among which approximately 54.6% belong to the R&D division. The results of operations are affected by the following company-specific factors. The results of operations depend significantly on Lotus Technology's ability to achieve vehicle delivery targets, which impacts vehicle sales revenue. It is critical for Lotus Technology to successfully manage production ramp-up and quality control, in cooperation with Geely Holding, to deliver vehicles to customers in targeted volume and of high quality. Currently, Lotus Technology works with a BEV manufacturing facility in Wuhan, China, which is owned and operated by Geely Holding.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Overview 
summary: We are a leading global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions, specializing in the design and development of luxury lifestyle vehicles under the esteemed British brand "Lotus." With a rich racing heritage spanning over 70 years, Lotus is renowned for its high-performance standards in both design and engineering. Since Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK in 2017, we have successfully transitioned into a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer, benefiting from Geely's support across various operational areas.

Our flagship model, the Eletre, is a luxury E-segment SUV that showcases our innovative 800-volt Electrical Performance Architecture (EPA). The Eletre is available in three versions: Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R. The Eletre R stands out with an impressive 905 horsepower, achieving acceleration from 0 to 100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds, and offers a WLTP range of 490 km, complemented by rapid charging capabilities. Deliveries of the Eletre commenced in 2023, and we launched our second model, the Emeya, in September 2023, with deliveries set to begin in March 2024. Furthermore, we have plans to introduce two additional models in the near future.

Our research and development capabilities, deeply rooted in Lotus's sports car legacy, serve as a significant competitive advantage, exemplified by the five-year development of the EPA, which facilitates rapid model rollout and enhances production efficiency. Additionally, we have developed an advanced Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS) featuring Level 4-ready hardware and a deployable LiDAR system.

To enhance customer experience, we employ a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, operating premium stores in high-traffic areas alongside a global digital platform for vehicle customization and purchasing. As of June 30, 2024, our global distribution network comprises 207 stores. In 2023, we delivered a total of 6,970 vehicles, with 4,873 vehicles delivered in the first half of 2024. Our workforce consists of 2,989 full-time employees, with a substantial 54.6% dedicated to research and development. 

This comprehensive approach positions us at the forefront of the luxury electric vehicle market, driving innovation and excellence in mobility solutions.

Additionally, Lotus Robotics, the company's division focused on intelligent driving solutions, is providing services not only for Lotus vehicles but also for other major automotive manufacturers, including Lynk&Co and Farizon Auto. and Full Year 2024 Financial Results

metadata.title_summary:
title: Operational and Financial Summaries 
summary: The operational results for Lotus Tech in the first quarter of 2024 (Q1 2024) reveal significant growth across various metrics compared to the same period in 2023 (Q1 2023). The company saw a remarkable increase in vehicle deliveries, with lifestyle vehicle deliveries soaring to 1,041 units—up 344% from 236 units in Q1 2023—and sportscar deliveries reaching 1,139 units, a substantial rise from just 28 units in the previous year. Overall, combined deliveries for both categories totaled 2,180 units, reflecting an impressive 731% year-over-year growth from 264 units in Q1 2023.

Financially, Lotus Tech reported a revenue of $173 million for Q1 2024, representing an 811% increase from $19 million in Q1 2023. However, the cost of revenues also increased significantly to $143 million, leading to a gross profit of $30 million and a gross profit margin of 18%, up from 1% in the prior year. Despite these gains, the company faced challenges with an operating loss of $233 million and a net loss of $258 million, both of which widened compared to the previous year. Adjusted EBITDA for Q1 2024 was negative $204 million, indicating a decline in profitability.

These results underscore Lotus Tech's strategic expansion into luxury markets and its commitment to innovation under the Vision80 plan, while also highlighting the ongoing challenges in achieving profitability amidst rapid growth and investment in global market engagement.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: New York – Oct 17, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the first nine months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 7,543 vehicles in the first nine months of 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of 134%. Global allocation in terms of deliveries remained well-distributed, with Europe, China, North America, and the Rest of the World regions each contributing about 20-35% of the total deliveries. In the third quarter of 2024, the Emeya (GT, Sedan) was delivered to Europe, whilst the Eletre (SUV) reached markets including the GCC, Japan, and Korea. In September, Lotus Technology Inc. revealed the Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant tailored for the North American market with a starting price of $229,900. The deliveries of the Eletre Carbon are expected to commence in the first half of 2025. Deliveries of bespoke and collection versions of both the Eletre (SUV) and the Emeya (GT, Sedan) also commenced in the third quarter.

title: Operating and Financial Results for the Second
Quarter of 2024 
summary: In the second quarter of 2024, the company achieved notable growth, delivering a total of 2,693 units, which represents a remarkable 128% increase compared to the same period in 2023. This growth was reflected in total revenues of $225 million, up 103% from $111 million a year earlier, alongside an improved gross margin of 9%, compared to 5% in Q2 2023. Despite these positive trends, the company reported a net loss of $202 million, a 4% increase from the previous year's loss, and an operating loss of $204 million, which also rose by 12%.

Breaking down the deliveries by model type, the "Lifestyle SUV and Sedan" category saw significant growth, with 1,342 units delivered, up from 637 units in Q2 2023, marking an increase of 111%. Similarly, "Sportscars" deliveries reached 1,337 units, a substantial rise from 538 units, reflecting a 149% year-over-year increase. 

Adjusted figures showed an adjusted net loss of $201 million and an adjusted EBITDA loss of $177 million, indicating a slight improvement from the previous year. The cost of revenues increased significantly to $204 million, highlighting the challenges the company continues to face despite overall growth. An earnings conference call is scheduled for August 28, 2024, to discuss these results in detail. All financial figures are rounded and unaudited.

The annual sales volume of Type 134 is expected to be between 80,000 and 90,000 units starting from 2027. Type 135 Type 135 is a BEV sports car planned for launch in 2025 with expected delivery in 2027.
